name: BacktrackPuzzle-v0
summary: GOAL is blocked by a wall gate. Navigate past it to reach a distant SWITCH, which opens the gate, then backtrack to collect the now-accessible GOAL.
objects: GOAL (initially blocked), SWITCH objects (trigger gate opening), WALL (gate barrier, opens after switch), dead-end paths.
goal: Activate the correct SWITCH to open the gate, then backtrack to reach GOAL.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Switches activate automatically when you step on them.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 L-shaped layout with 1 switch. No dead ends. Clear backtrack path.
11x11 T-shaped layout with 2 switches and 1 dead end.
13x13 zigzag layout with 3 switches and 2 dead ends.
15x15 complex layout with 4 switches and 3 dead ends. Multiple gates.

name: ChaseEvade-v0
summary: Survive against color-coded ENEMY NPCs with diverse behaviors inspired by Pacman ghosts. Chaser (red, BFS pursuit), Ambusher (pink, targets ahead of agent), Patrol (cyan, fixed route), Erratic (orange, random movement). SWITCH power-ups freeze all enemies for 5 steps. Success = surviving all required steps.
objects: ENEMY NPCs (color-coded: Chaser/Ambusher/Patrol/Erratic), SWITCH objects (freeze all enemies for 5 steps, one-time use), WALL (obstacles).
goal: Survive the required steps without enemy collision.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Evade enemies using obstacles and freeze switches strategically.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 1 chaser, survive 30 steps, 1 freeze switch. Open layout.
10x10 grid, 1 chaser + 1 ambusher, survive 60 steps, 1 freeze switch, 3 obstacles.
13x13 grid, 1 chaser + 1 ambusher + 1 patrol, survive 100 steps, 2 freeze switches, 5 obstacles.
15x15 grid, 1 chaser + 1 ambusher + 1 patrol + 1 erratic, survive 160 steps, 2 freeze switches, 7 obstacles.

name: CooperativeTransport-v0
summary: Push heavy boxes into holes with NPC cooperation. Boxes are too heavy to push alone; the NPC must be adjacent to the same box for a push to succeed. The NPC wanders randomly but pathfinds to the opposite side of the box when the agent is adjacent.
objects: BOX (heavy, requires NPC cooperation to push), HOLE terrain (destination for boxes), NPC helper (must be adjacent for push), WALL (obstacles).
goal: Push all heavy boxes into holes with NPC cooperation.
actions: Move in 4 directions into BOX to push it. Push only works when NPC is also adjacent to the same box. Box pushed onto HOLE removes both box and hole.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 1 box, 1 hole, no obstacles.
12x12 grid, 2 boxes, 2 holes, 3 obstacles.
15x15 grid, 3 boxes, 3 holes, 6 obstacles.
18x18 grid, 4 boxes, 4 holes, 9 obstacles.

name: CuriosityMaze-v0
summary: Explore a procedurally generated maze and visit a target percentage of all reachable cells within a step budget. No targets or landmarks — success is purely coverage-based. The agent must remember where it has been.
objects: WALL (border and random interior walls forming maze structure). No target objects or landmarks on the grid.
goal: Visit at least the required percentage of all reachable cells before the step budget runs out.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Each new cell visited counts toward coverage. No visual markers for visited/unvisited — the agent must track its own path.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 maze, 70% coverage needed, 80 steps. Low wall density.
13x13 maze, 75% coverage needed, 150 steps. Moderate wall density.
17x17 maze, 80% coverage needed, 280 steps. High wall density.
21x21 maze, 85% coverage needed, 450 steps. Dense complex maze.

name: DeceptiveReward-v0
summary: Misleading reward gradient. Decoy paths with COIN chains lead to TARGET objects (touching TARGET = failure, no success). True path has no visible rewards and leads to the real GOAL. Collecting ANY coin from a decoy path closes the gate to the true path (WALL placed). Dense reward intentionally points toward decoy coins.
objects: COIN objects (decoy reward chains along branching paths), TARGET (decoy endpoint, touching = failure), GOAL (true objective, on reward-free path), KEY and DOOR (hard+, gate the true path).
goal: Resist the coin reward gradient. Navigate the true (reward-free) path to GOAL without collecting any coins.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Coins are auto-collected on contact. Collecting a coin permanently closes the true path gate. Step on TARGET = failure.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 1 decoy path, 3 coins. Direct true path, no keys.
11x11 grid, 2 decoy paths, 5 coins each. True path has 1 turn.
13x13 grid, 3 decoy paths, 7 coins each. True path behind 1 key+door.
15x15 grid, 4 decoy paths, 9 coins each. True path behind 2 keys+doors.

name: DelayedGratification-v0
summary: Resist nearby decoy KEY objects (traps) and navigate to a distant true GOAL. Collecting a decoy KEY blocks future success and ends the episode. Tests impulse control.
objects: GOAL (true objective, distant), KEY objects (decoy traps, closer to start), WALL (optional obstacles), HAZARD (hard+, blocks paths).
goal: Reach the distant true GOAL without collecting any decoy KEY. Collecting a decoy ends episode.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Avoid stepping on KEY objects which act as traps.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid with 2 decoy KEYs. Open layout, clear path to goal.
10x10 grid with 4 decoy KEYs, 4 walls. Must navigate around traps.
13x13 maze with 6 decoys, 8 walls, 3 hazards. Hazards block shortcuts.
15x15 complex maze with 8 decoys, 12 walls, 6 hazards. Careful long-range planning.

name: DistributionShift-v0
summary: Multi-phase maze navigation: reach 3 GOAL positions across shifting DFS mazes. After each goal, the maze regenerates with a new layout and goal position. Hard+ adds wall-toggling (walls appear/disappear between phases). Expert adds action remapping (UP<->DOWN, LEFT<->RIGHT). No enemies at any difficulty.
objects: GOAL (1 per phase, 3 total), WALL (DFS maze, toggling walls at hard+), KEY/DOOR (hard+, color-coded), action remap (expert, UP<->DOWN LEFT<->RIGHT).
goal: Reach all 3 goals across shifting maze phases.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Pick up KEYs to unlock DOORs. Maze layout and wall configuration change between phases. At expert, action controls flip.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 DFS maze, 3 goals, maze shifts between phases. max_steps=200.
11x11 DFS maze, 3 goals, maze shifts. max_steps=350.
13x13 DFS maze, 3 goals, 1 key-door, wall-toggling between phases. max_steps=500.
15x15 DFS maze, 3 goals, 2 key-doors, wall-toggling + action remap. max_steps=700.

name: DynamicObstacles-v0
summary: Navigate toward a GOAL while avoiding moving NPC obstacles that patrol the grid. NPCs move probabilistically each step; expert mode adds pursuit behavior.
objects: GOAL (static destination), NPC objects (moving obstacles, fatal on collision), WALL (static barriers).
goal: Reach GOAL without colliding with any NPC. Collision ends episode.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Timing and prediction of NPC movement is key.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid with 2 NPCs (50% move chance). Open layout, easy avoidance.
10x10 grid with 3 NPCs (75% move chance) and 3 scattered walls.
13x13 grid with 5 NPCs (100% move) and 6 wall obstacles.
15x15 grid with 7 NPCs (90% move, 15% pursuit), 9 walls. Pursuing behavior.

name: EmergentStrategy-v0
summary: GOAL is behind horizontal WALL barriers opened by locking pressure plates (TARGET). Four color-coded NPC types with unified discoverable behaviors: Follower (cyan, BFS toward cell behind agent), Fearful (green, flees from agent within distance 2), Mirror (purple, moves toward mirrored agent position), Contrarian (gold, moves opposite to agent's last action). When an NPC steps on a plate, it LOCKS permanently and the barrier stays open.
objects: TARGET (locking pressure plates, metadata stores barrier index), WALL barriers (full-width horizontal rows, open permanently when plate locked), color-coded NPCs (Follower/Fearful/Mirror/Contrarian), GOAL (behind last barrier).
goal: Exploit NPC behaviors to lure/scare them onto locking pressure plates, permanently opening barriers to reach the GOAL in the final zone.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Cannot walk through NPCs. Follower stays 1 tile behind you; Fearful flees from you; Mirror mirrors your position; Contrarian moves opposite to you. Guide NPCs onto plates to lock them.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 1 locking plate, 1 follower. Single barrier.
12x12 grid, 2 locking plates, 1 follower + 1 fearful. Two barriers.
15x15 grid, 3 locking plates, 1 follower + 1 fearful + 1 contrarian. Three barriers.
18x18 grid, 4 locking plates, 1 follower + 1 fearful + 1 contrarian + 1 mirror. Four barriers.

name: FewShotAdaptation-v0
summary: K auto-advancing demo trials followed by 1 test trial. Each trial places N candidate objects (GEM, SCROLL, ORB, COIN). A hidden rule determines the correct candidate: goto_type, nearest_corner, furthest_start, or most_open (hard+). Demo trials briefly highlight the correct candidate as GOAL, then revert. Test trial: agent must navigate to the correct candidate. Wrong choice = failure.
objects: Candidate objects (GEM, SCROLL, ORB, COIN), GOAL highlight (shown briefly during demos, reverts after reveal_steps), WALL (obstacles at medium+).
goal: Watch demo trials to infer the hidden rule, then navigate to the correct candidate object in the test trial. Stepping on wrong candidate = failure.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Demo trials auto-advance (no interaction). In the test trial, step on a candidate object to select it.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 2 candidates, 2 demos, 12-step reveal. Rules: goto_type, nearest_corner.
9x9 grid, 3 candidates, 2 demos, 8-step reveal, 4 obstacles.
11x11 grid, 3 candidates, 2 demos, 5-step reveal, 6 obstacles. Adds most_open rule.
13x13 grid, 4 candidates, 3 demos, 3-step reveal, 8 obstacles. All 4 rules.

name: FogOfWarExploration-v0
summary: Explore a grid with limited visibility (fog of war). The agent can only see within a small radius and must remember explored areas to find the hidden GOAL.
objects: GOAL (hidden behind fog), WALL (revealed in view), HAZARD (dangerous), Guard NPCs (hard+), Decoy GOALs (medium+), FOG (unrevealed cells).
goal: Find and reach the GOAL despite incomplete map information.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Fog reveals cells within visibility radius as you move.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, visibility radius 2. No hazards, guards, or decoys. Systematic exploration.
10x10 grid, visibility 2, 2 decoy goals. Memory of explored areas needed.
13x13 grid, visibility 1 (tight fog), 1 guard, 3 decoys. Dense layout.
15x15 grid, visibility 1, 2 guards, 4 decoys. Extreme memory demand.

name: GoToGoal-v0
summary: Navigate a grid to reach a visible GOAL while avoiding walls, hazards, and patrolling guard NPCs.
objects: GOAL (destination), WALL (obstacles), HAZARD (lava, fatal), Guard NPCs (patrolling, collision penalty), Decoy GOALs (false targets).
goal: Reach the GOAL position. Stepping on HAZARD or colliding with a guard ends the episode.
actions: Move in 4 directions (up/down/left/right). No interact action needed.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 5x5 open grid, no walls, guards, or hazards. Straightforward path to goal.
10x10 grid with partial walls and 1 guard. No decoys. Requires obstacle avoidance.
15x15 dense maze with 2 guards, 2 decoys, and 4 scattered hazards. Complex routing.
20x20 complex maze with 3 guards, 4 decoys, and 8 hazards. Maximum challenge.

name: GraphColoring-v0
summary: Color graph nodes via INTERACT action to cycle through colors. Nodes are SWITCH objects placed in clusters with connected adjacency ensuring non-trivial constraints. No adjacent nodes may share color.
objects: SWITCH objects (graph nodes, cluster-based placement, color state in metadata), WALL (obstacles).
goal: Color all nodes so no two adjacent nodes share the same color.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Use INTERACT (action 5) on a node to cycle its color.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 4 nodes, 2 colors, linear graph. Simple constraint.
11x11 grid, 6 nodes, 3 colors, cycle graph with cluster-based adjacency.
13x13 grid, 8 nodes, 3 colors, connected clusters with 4 obstacles.
15x15 grid, 10 nodes, 4 colors, dense connected clusters with 6 obstacles.

name: Herding-v0
summary: Herd NPC SHEEP into a pen zone (TARGET cells) using movement pressure. Sheep flee from the agent when within distance 2. Predators scatter sheep at hard+.
objects: SHEEP NPCs (flee from agent), pen zone (TARGET cells), leader sheep (hard+, influence herd), ENEMY predators (scatter sheep at hard+), WALL (obstacles).
goal: Move all SHEEP into the pen zone (TARGET cells).
actions: Move in 4 directions. Your position influences sheep movement; approach from the opposite side to push them toward the pen.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 2 sheep, 2x2 pen. No obstacles or predators. Simple herding.
12x12 grid, 3 sheep, 3x3 pen, 2 obstacles, random pen corner.
15x15 grid, 4 sheep, 3x3 pen, 4 obstacles, 1 predator, 1 leader sheep.
18x18 grid, 5 sheep, 3x3 pen, 6 obstacles, 2 predators, 2 leader sheep.

name: InstructionFollowing-v0
summary: Navigate to the one target object (GEM, SCROLL, ORB, or COIN) while avoiding distractor objects of different types. Touching a distractor ends the episode with penalty. Hard+ adds key-and-door rooms gating the target.
objects: Target object (one of GEM/SCROLL/ORB/COIN), distractor objects (other types), KEY and DOOR (hard+, color-coded).
goal: Reach the unique target object without touching any distractor. Hard+ requires collecting a key to unlock the door.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Stepping on a KEY auto-collects it. Walking into a DOOR with the matching key opens it.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 1 target, 3 distractors, no doors.
10x10 grid, 1 target, 6 distractors, no doors.
13x13 grid, 1 target, 8 distractors, 1 key-door room.
15x15 grid, 1 target, 12 distractors, 2 key-door rooms.

name: KeyDoorPuzzle-v0
summary: Collect color-coded keys and unlock ALL matching doors to reach the GOAL in the innermost room. One-key-at-a-time inventory limit: picking up a new key drops any held key. At hard+, keys are placed in previous rooms, forcing backtracking through already-opened doors.
objects: KEY objects (color-coded: gold/red/blue/green, one-at-a-time inventory), DOOR (opens with matching-color key), GOAL (in final room behind ALL doors), Guard NPCs (hard+), WALL (room separators and corridors).
goal: Reach GOAL after unlocking ALL doors with matching keys. Every door must be opened.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Keys are picked up automatically when you step on them (drops any currently held key). Walk into a DOOR with the matching key to unlock it.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid with 1 color-coded key-door pair. No guards. Key in hub, goal behind the single door.
11x11 grid with 2 color-coded key-door pairs. No guards. Key for door 2 is behind door 1, forcing backtracking.
13x13 grid with 3 chained color-coded locks, 1 guard. Keys placed in previous rooms, forcing backtracking.
15x15 grid with 4 chained color-coded locks, 2 guards. Complex key dependencies with keys in earlier rooms requiring multi-room backtracking.

name: LightsOut-v0
summary: Toggle SWITCH objects (lights) to turn them all OFF. Easy mode: each switch toggles only itself. Medium+: toggling a light also toggles its 4 adjacent neighbors.
objects: SWITCH objects (lights, ON/OFF state visible via color), decoy switches (medium+, outside puzzle grid).
goal: Turn all lights OFF by toggling switches.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Step on a SWITCH to toggle it (and adjacent neighbors at medium+).

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 3x3 puzzle, self-only toggle. Each switch is independent.
9x9 grid, 4x4 puzzle, adjacent toggle (self + 4 neighbors), 1 decoy.
11x11 grid, 4x4 puzzle, adjacent toggle, 2 decoys.
13x13 grid, 5x5 puzzle, adjacent toggle, 3 decoys, random initial state.

name: MazeNavigation-v0
summary: Solve procedurally generated mazes (binary tree or recursive backtracker) to reach the exit GOAL. At medium+, colored key/door pairs block the optimal path at choke points and must be collected/opened. At hard+, hazard terrain appears in dead ends. No guard NPCs.
objects: GOAL (maze exit), WALL (maze structure), KEY objects (collect to unlock doors, medium+), DOOR (blocks path, opens with matching key, medium+), HAZARD (dead ends, hard+).
goal: Navigate the maze to reach the GOAL exit. Collect keys and open doors blocking the path.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Keys are picked up automatically. Walk into a DOOR with the matching key to open it.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 binary-tree maze. No keys or hazards. Simple pathfinding exercise.
11x11 recursive-backtracker maze, 1 key-door pair blocking the path.
15x15 recursive-backtracker maze, 2 key-door pairs, hazard terrain in dead ends.
21x21 dense recursive maze, 3 key-door pairs, hazards. Maximum branching.

name: NoisyObservation-v0
summary: Find the true GOAL hidden among heavy visual noise: decoy TARGETs, ghost objects that appear/disappear each step, moving decoys, and patrolling guards.
objects: True GOAL (single), decoy TARGETs (look similar), ghost objects (SCROLL/ORB/LEVER appearing/disappearing), moving decoys (hard+), Guard NPCs (medium+).
goal: Locate and reach the true GOAL amid visual noise.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Identify the true GOAL among decoys and noise.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 3 decoys, 2 ghosts per step. No guards. Low noise level.
9x9 grid, 6 decoys, 4 ghosts, 1 guard. Moderate noise.
11x11 maze, 10 decoys, 6 ghosts, 2 guards, 4 moving decoys.
13x13 grid, 14 decoys, 10 ghosts, 3 guards, 6 moving decoys. Extreme noise.

name: PackingPuzzle-v0
summary: Push typed pieces (BOX, GEM, ORB, SCROLL) onto matching TARGET slots. Each target requires a specific piece type. Distractor pieces add noise.
objects: Piece objects (BOX, GEM, ORB, SCROLL, typed), TARGET slots (with required type metadata), distractor pieces (unused types, medium+).
goal: Push each piece onto its matching-type target slot.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Walk into a piece to push it. Match piece type to target type.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 2 pieces of 2 types, 2 targets. No distractors, open layout.
9x9 grid, 3 pieces of 2 types, 3 targets, 1 distractor.
11x11 maze, 4 pieces of 3 types, 4 targets, 1 distractor.
13x13 grid, 5 pieces of 4 types, 5 targets, 2 distractors. Complex maze.

name: PreciseNavigation-v0
summary: Ice sliding puzzle on larger grids with interior L/T/I-shaped wall segments. The interior is filled with ICE terrain; the agent slides until hitting a WALL, an EMPTY cell (stopping point), or a BOX. Fewer stopping points and minimum solution path length validation ensure non-trivial slide sequences. BOX objects at hard+ also slide when pushed.
objects: ICE terrain (agent slides across), EMPTY cells (stopping points), WALL (border and interior L/T/I-shaped segments), GOAL (on an EMPTY cell), BOX (pushable, slides on ice at hard+).
goal: Slide across ice to reach the GOAL by planning trajectories through stopping points.
actions: Move in 4 directions. On ICE, the agent slides until hitting a wall, EMPTY cell, or BOX. On EMPTY, movement is normal. Push BOX objects (hard+) by sliding into them.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, fewer stopping points, interior wall segments. 3-4 slides to reach goal.
11x11 grid, fewer stopping points, L/T-shaped walls. 5-6 slides to reach goal.
13x13 grid, sparse stopping points, 1 pushable box. 7-9 slides.
15x15 grid, minimal stopping points, 2 pushable boxes. 10-14 slides.

name: ProgramSynthesis-v0
summary: Replicate a reference SCROLL pattern by pushing scattered GEM objects (Sokoban-style) into the same relative shape. The reference pattern (line, L, T, or cross) is shown as immovable SCROLL objects. Matching is translation-invariant: GEMs must form the same normalized offsets as SCROLLs, anywhere on the map.
objects: SCROLL objects (immovable reference pattern), GEM objects (pushable, Sokoban-style), WALL (obstacles). No TARGET markers — GEMs just need to match the SCROLL shape.
goal: Push all GEM objects so they form the same relative pattern as the reference SCROLLs.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Walk into a GEM to push it one cell in your movement direction. Cannot push gems into walls, other gems, or SCROLL blocks.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, line pattern, 3 gems to push.
11x11 grid, L-shape pattern, 4 gems.
13x13 grid, T-shape pattern, 5 gems.
15x15 grid, cross pattern, 6 gems.

name: RecipeAssembly-v0
summary: Follow a recipe: collect ingredients in exact order and deliver each to the crafting station. Recipe is displayed visually in corner zone using TARGET markers.
objects: Recipe zone (TARGET objects with ingredient type metadata), ingredient items (GEM, SCROLL, ORB, COIN), crafting station (NPC object), decoy ingredients, WALL.
goal: Collect and deliver all ingredients in recipe order to the crafting station.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Ingredients are picked up automatically. Step on crafting station to deliver the held ingredient.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 2-ingredient recipe. No decoys, open layout. Simple collect-deliver.
11x11 grid, 3-ingredient recipe, 2 decoys, 3 obstacles. Plan route around walls.
14x14 maze, 4-ingredient recipe, 3 decoys, 5 obstacles.
16x16 dense maze, 5-ingredient recipe, 4 decoys, 7 obstacles.

name: RecursiveRooms-v0
summary: Navigate recursively subdivided nested rooms. Grid is divided into quadrants with walls; each quadrant subdivides further. GOAL is in the deepest nested room.
objects: Recursive room structure (walls and doorways), doorways (EMPTY gaps in walls, 3 of 4 sides open), GOAL (deepest nested room), sealed doors (1 side per level).
goal: Navigate through nested rooms to reach GOAL in the deepest room.
actions: Move in 4 directions through doorways between rooms.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 13x13 grid, depth 2 (4 rooms), 3 doorways per level.
19x19 grid, depth 3 (16 rooms), 3 doorways per level.
25x25 grid, depth 4 (64 rooms), 3 doorways, goal in random deepest room.
31x31 grid, depth 4 (64 rooms), sealed doorways, deep random goal.

name: ResourceManagement-v0
summary: Keep energy stations alive by recharging them before they drain to zero. Stations drain energy over time. Any station dying ends the episode in failure. Success = surviving all max_steps.
objects: RESOURCE stations (energy 0-100, drain over time), WALL (obstacles between stations).
goal: Keep ALL stations above 0 energy for the entire episode (survive max_steps). Any station reaching 0 = failure.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Step on a RESOURCE station to recharge it to full (100).

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 2 stations, slow drain (every 8 steps). No obstacles.
12x12 grid, 3 stations, moderate drain (every 5 steps), 3 obstacles.
15x15 grid, 4 stations, fast drain (every 3 steps), 5 obstacles.
18x18 grid, 5 stations, very fast drain (every 2 steps), 7 obstacles, far apart.

name: RuleInduction-v0
summary: Discover which SWITCH objects are real by observing terrain patterns (ICE south of real switches), then INTERACT to activate them in any order. Activating all real switches opens a barrier DOOR to the GOAL. Activating a wrong (decoy) switch deactivates the most recently activated real switch, penalizing trial-and-error.
objects: SWITCH (real: ICE south; decoy: no ICE south), ICE terrain cues, GEM hints (easy), DOOR (barrier, opens when all real switches active), GOAL (behind barrier), WALL.
goal: Identify real switches via ICE pattern, INTERACT all real ones, pass barrier to GOAL.
actions: Move in 4 directions. INTERACT on a switch to toggle it. Wrong switches undo your last correct activation.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 2 real + 1 decoy switches, GEM hints + ICE pattern.
11x11 grid, 3 real + 2 decoy switches, ICE pattern only.
13x13 grid, 4 real + 3 decoy switches, ICE pattern only.
15x15 grid, 5 real + 4 decoy switches, ICE pattern only.

name: SequenceMemory-v0
summary: Two-phase task: show phase displays target positions as GEM objects one by one. Reproduce phase requires visiting those memorized positions in exact order. No visual markers during reproduce — pure spatial memory.
objects: GEM objects (shown during show phase, then removed), distractor positions (invalid positions, medium+, no visual marker — tracked internally only).
goal: Memorize shown GEM positions, then visit them in exact order during reproduce phase.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Walk to each memorized position in order. Visiting a wrong position (distractor) incurs a penalty.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 3 targets shown for 4 steps each. No distractors. Short sequence.
10x10 grid, 4 targets shown for 3 steps each, 2 distractor positions, 4 obstacles.
13x13 grid, 5 targets shown for 2 steps each, 3 distractors, shuffled order.
15x15 grid, 6 targets shown for 1 step each, 4 distractors, shuffled order.

name: ShortestPath-v0
summary: Visit multiple GOAL objects scattered across the grid in any order. Tests multi-objective pathfinding similar to the traveling salesman problem. Success requires visiting all goals within a step budget relative to the optimal path length.
objects: GOAL objects (multiple, consumed on visit), WALL (obstacles), TARGET objects (decoy false targets, medium+).
goal: Visit all real GOAL objects within the step budget (optimal path × budget multiplier).
actions: Move in 4 directions. Step on GOAL to collect it automatically. Avoid decoy TARGETs.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 open grid with 2 goals and no obstacles. Simple multi-stop navigation.
10x10 grid with 3 goals, 3 obstacles, and 1 decoy TARGET. Route planning required.
13x13 grid with 4 goals, 5 obstacles, and 2 decoy TARGETs. Complex routing needed.
15x15 maze-like grid with 5 goals, 8 obstacles, and 3 decoy TARGETs.

name: SokobanPush-v0
summary: Classic Sokoban: push BOX objects onto TARGET positions by walking into them. Boxes move in the direction the agent pushes. Cannot push into walls.
objects: BOX objects (pushable), TARGET positions (where boxes must go), WALL (immovable obstacles).
goal: Push all BOX objects onto matching TARGET positions.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Walk into a BOX to push it in your movement direction. Cannot push boxes into walls or other boxes.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid with 1 box and 1 target. Open layout, no dead-end traps.
10x10 grid with 2 boxes, 2 targets, 3 obstacles creating push constraints.
13x13 grid with 3 boxes, 3 targets, 5 obstacles, 3 hazards.
15x15 grid with 4 boxes, 4 targets, 8 obstacles, 5 hazards. Complex mazes.

name: SwitchCircuit-v0
summary: Non-linear switch dependency puzzle. An open arena has N switches toggled via INTERACT action, each controlling wall-segment barriers. Switches have mutual exclusion dependencies: activating one may deactivate another. No number annotations on switches — the agent must discover dependencies through experimentation.
objects: SWITCH objects (color-coded, toggled via INTERACT), WALL barrier segments (1-3 cells, metadata = barrier index), GOAL (behind final barrier).
goal: Plan switch activation order to open all barriers blocking the path to GOAL.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Use INTERACT (action 5) on a SWITCH to toggle it ON/OFF. Mutual exclusion: toggling one switch may deactivate another.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 2 switches. Simple dependency: A opens path to B, B opens goal.
11x11 grid, 3 switches. One mutual exclusion requires planning order.
13x13 grid, 4 switches. Multiple mutual exclusions, backtracking needed.
15x15 grid, 5 switches. Complex dependency graph with mutual exclusions.

name: SymbolMatching-v0
summary: Match symbol items (GEM, POTION, SCROLL, COIN, ORB, LEVER) to their matching targets of the same type. Items are on the left half; matching targets (same ObjectType) are on the right half. Pick up an item and deliver it to the matching target. Placing on wrong type = mismatch penalty.
objects: Symbol items on left side (GEM/POTION/SCROLL/COIN/ORB/LEVER), matching targets on right side (same ObjectType as their item), fake items (type with no matching target, medium+).
goal: Pick up each symbol item and deliver it to the matching target of the same type on the right side of the grid.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Step on a symbol item to pick it up (one at a time). Step on a matching target (same symbol type) to deliver it.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid with 2 symbol-target pairs. No fake items. Clear pairing.
10x10 grid with 3 pairs, 1 fake item, 3 obstacles.
13x13 maze with 4 pairs, 2 fake items, 5 obstacles.
15x15 grid with 5 pairs, 3 fake items, 8 obstacles.

name: TagHunt-v0
summary: Tag all fleeing NPCs before time runs out. NPCs evade the agent. SWITCH objects freeze all NPCs for 5 steps when activated (one-time use).
objects: ENEMY (fleeing NPCs), SWITCH (freeze power-up, one-time use), WALL (obstacles).
goal: Tag all NPCs by stepping onto them. Use freeze switches strategically.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Step on ENEMY to tag it. Step on SWITCH to freeze all NPCs for 5 steps.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 1 NPC (50% evade), 1 freeze switch, no obstacles.
10x10 grid, 2 NPCs (65% evade), 1 freeze switch, 3 obstacles.
13x13 grid, 3 NPCs (80% evade), 1 freeze switch, 5 obstacles.
15x15 grid, 4 NPCs (90% evade), 1 freeze switch, 7 obstacles.

name: TaskInterference-v0
summary: Resource Tug-of-War: two meters (GEM and ORB) start at 0.0 and must both reach a threshold. GEM items raise GEM meter +0.25 but cross-drain ORB; ORB items raise ORB meter +0.25 but cross-drain GEM. Cross-drain is 0.10 at easy/medium, 0.15 at hard+. At hard+: consecutive same-type penalty (extra -0.05 drain). At medium+: items flee from agent every 3 steps. At hard+: both meters decay 0.005/step.
objects: GEM objects (metadata=1), ORB objects (metadata=2). Items scattered on open floor. At medium+ items move away from agent every 3 steps.
goal: Raise both GEM and ORB meters to >= threshold simultaneously. Alternate collection to avoid cross-drain and consecutive penalty.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Items auto-collected on contact. Collecting raises one meter and cross-drains the other.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9, 4 gem + 4 orb, threshold=0.6, 0.10 cross-drain, items stationary.
11x11, 5+5, threshold=0.7, 0.10 cross-drain, items flee every 3 steps.
13x13, 8+8, threshold=0.8, 0.15 cross-drain, consecutive penalty, 0.005 decay/step.
15x15, 10+10, threshold=0.8, 0.15 cross-drain, consecutive penalty, 0.005 decay/step.

name: TileSorting-v0
summary: Sliding tile puzzle: arrange numbered tiles into correct order by pushing them into the empty slot. Classic 15-puzzle mechanic with varying sizes.
objects: Numbered tiles (1..N-1 with unique numbers/colors), empty slot (agent position), target positions marked on floor showing goal arrangement.
goal: Arrange tiles to goal configuration (1,2,3...N-1 in row-major order).
actions: Move in 4 directions into a tile to swap positions with it (push into empty slot).

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 2x2 puzzle (3 tiles), 5 scrambles. Few moves to solve.
9x9 grid, 3x3 puzzle (8 tiles), 15 scrambles.
11x11 grid, 3x3 puzzle (8 tiles), 30 scrambles. Heavy scrambling.
13x13 grid, 4x4 puzzle (15 tiles), 60 scrambles. Maximum depth.

name: TimingChallenge-v0
summary: Cross a gap blocked by a moving BLOCKER patrol. Time your movement to pass through when the BLOCKER is not in the way.
objects: BLOCKER (moving patrol obstacle), gap zone (patrol area), safe refuge spots (hard+), GOAL (beyond patrol zone), WALL (boundaries).
goal: Cross the patrol zone without collision, then reach GOAL. Collision ends episode.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Wait for BLOCKER to move away, then cross quickly.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 7x7 grid, 1 slow blocker, 1 gap. No safe spots. Simple timing.
9x9 grid, 2 blockers, 1 gap. Random gap position.
11x11 grid, 2 blockers with speed variation, 2 gaps, 1 safe spot.
13x13 grid, 3 blockers with variable speed, 2 gaps, 2 safe spots.

name: ToolUse-v0
summary: GOAL is behind a horizontal river (WATER band). Agent can cross the river but only gets partial reward without the ORB tool. SCROLL objects are scattered on the agent's side. Collecting ALL required scrolls spawns an ORB. Picking up the ORB and then reaching the GOAL gives full reward. COIN decoys look different and have no effect.
objects: SCROLL (collectible clue), COIN (decoy, no effect), ORB (spawns after all scrolls collected), WATER (river band), GOAL (on far side of river).
goal: Collect all SCROLLs to spawn the ORB, pick up the ORB, cross the river, and reach the GOAL for full reward (1.0). Without the ORB, reward is only 0.2.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Scrolls, coins, and the ORB are picked up automatically when you step on them. The ORB only appears after all scrolls are collected.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 2 scrolls, 0 decoys, 1 river (2 cells wide).
11x11 grid, 3 scrolls, 1 decoy, 1 river (3 cells wide).
13x13 grid, 4 scrolls, 2 decoys, 2 river crossings (2 cells wide).
15x15 grid, 5 scrolls, 3 decoys, 2 river crossings (3 cells wide).

name: TreasureHunt-v0
summary: Find invisible hidden treasures by reading directional SCROLL clues. Scrolls encode the direction (N/E/S/W) and distance to the nearest hidden treasure as metadata (direction * 10 + distance). Stepping on a scroll reads and consumes it. Some clues are misleading at harder difficulties.
objects: SCROLL objects (directional clues, consumed on contact), invisible treasures (not rendered — step on exact cell to collect), WALL (obstacles).
goal: Read scroll clues, triangulate hidden treasure positions, and step on each treasure cell to collect all treasures.
actions: Move in 4 directions. Step on SCROLL to read it (consumed). Step on a hidden treasure cell to collect it. Metadata encodes direction*10+distance.

difficulty_descriptions.description: 9x9 grid, 2 treasures, 6 scrolls, 0 misleading. Simple triangulation.
12x12 grid, 3 treasures, 8 scrolls, 1 misleading. Must cross-check clues.
15x15 grid, 4 treasures, 10 scrolls, 3 misleading. Significant deception.
18x18 grid, 5 treasures, 12 scrolls, 5 misleading. Nearly half the clues lie.
